title: [apiv2] command can't be executed correctly by attached running container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2.  to run a container

3. to attach the above running container and execute command

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -X POST http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/attach?stdin=1&stdout=1
[1] 104589
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ *   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/attach?stdin=1 HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 101 UPGRADED
< Content-Type: application/vnd.docker.raw-stream
< Connection: Upgrade
< Upgrade: tcp
<
pwd
/home/fedora/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ pwd
/home/fedora/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod

NOTE: command is executed in host namespace not inside the container

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman exec myalpine pwd
/
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
command is executed inside the container and output correct result
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         4611ff5ce4ff67a142363ab66e10944bfc40f860
Built:              Fri May 15 03:26:27 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0-dev

  idMappings:

      host_id: 100000

  kernel: 5.6.10-300.fc32.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 715292672
  memTotal: 4118323200
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.0.0-1.fc32.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.0
      commit: a3be729152a33e692cd28b52f664defbf2e7810a
      libslirp: 4.2.0
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 171h 35m 36.9s (Approximately 7.12 days)

  - registry.fedoraproject.org

  graphDriverName: overlay

    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.0.0
        FUSE library version 3.9.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ git rev-parse HEAD
4611ff5ce4ff67a142363ab66e10944bfc40f860
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Openstack VM

comments.body:
I don't think we can test attach with curl, and I'm 99% certain that your command is actually running in the host's shell, not being forwarded to the container. The URL itself seems to have some escaping issues.

I can see streaming output from a container started as `podman run -t -i --rm --name testctr alpine top` with `sudo curl --unix-socket /var/run/podman/podman.sock -v -X POST 'http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/testctr/attach'` but nothing I type is forwarded to the container - it seems like a curl issue, potentially? This is also the best-case scenario - a container without `-t` will produce unreadable binary gibberish that cannot be interpreted by curl.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> I don't think we can test attach with curl, and I'm 99% certain that your command is actually running in the host's shell, not being forwarded to the container. The URL itself seems to have some escaping issues.
> 
> I can see streaming output from a container started as `podman run -t -i --rm --name testctr alpine top` with `sudo curl --unix-socket /var/run/podman/podman.sock -v -X POST 'http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/testctr/attach'` but nothing I type is forwarded to the container - it seems like a curl issue, potentially? This is also the best-case scenario - a container without `-t` will produce unreadable binary gibberish that cannot be interpreted by curl.

@mheon what is the best way to test this scenario? I agree curl is not a good option.

I'm going to recommend closing this issue. As reported, it is a problem with the reporter's setup, not podman: the reporter is failing to use quotes in the curl command, which of course makes the shell interpret `&` as 'background me'.

curl works perfectly fine with attach and stdout, it just (unsurprisingly) does not handle stdin:
```console
$ podman run -d --name foo alpine_labels top
$ curl -X POST -o - 'http://localhost:9999/v1.40/libpod/containers/foo/attach?stdin=1&stdout=1'
[pause for a second, then clears screen and displays the running 'top' output]
```
It works fine with `nc` but devising an actually useful test of this is left as an exercise for the reader:
```console
$ podman run -d -it --name foo alpine_labels sh                                                                                                 -
b3b5b52f753abea712a8be565c78078c61405a1217f17aa6b652111aaaf13197
$ nc localhost 9999
POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/foo/attach?stdin=1&stdout=1 HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost

HTTP/1.1 101 UPGRADED
Content-Type: application/vnd.docker.raw-stream
Connection: Upgrade
Upgrade: tcp

date
date
Wed Jul  8 16:52:41 UTC 2020
/ # ^[[60;5Rs
```

> I'm going to recommend closing this issue. As reported, it is a problem with the reporter's setup, not podman: the reporter is failing to use quotes in the curl command, which of course makes the shell interpret `&` as 'background me'.
> 
> curl works perfectly fine with attach and stdout, it just (unsurprisingly) does not handle stdin:
> 
> ```
> $ podman run -d --name foo alpine_labels top
> $ curl -X POST -o - 'http://localhost:9999/v1.40/libpod/containers/foo/attach?stdin=1&stdout=1'
> [pause for a second, then clears screen and displays the running 'top' output]
> ```
> 
> It works fine with `nc` but devising an actually useful test of this is left as an exercise for the reader:
> 
> ```
> $ podman run -d -it --name foo alpine_labels sh                                                                                                 -
> b3b5b52f753abea712a8be565c78078c61405a1217f17aa6b652111aaaf13197
> $ nc localhost 9999
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/foo/attach?stdin=1&stdout=1 HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost
> 
> HTTP/1.1 101 UPGRADED
> Content-Type: application/vnd.docker.raw-stream
> Connection: Upgrade
> Upgrade: tcp
> 
> date
> date
> Wed Jul  8 16:52:41 UTC 2020
> / # ^[[60;5Rs
> ```

Understood, thanks for the answer!
